description:
Close the arc by deleting the retired direct definition-publication machinery, shrinking scope publication to alias/import/require plus item-macro expansion and compiler callback application, and making the compiler prelude use the real callback path instead of a side channel. Update docs, telemetry, and tests so the staged boundary is explicit and enforceable.

This is the deletion and closeout ticket: remove obsolete reserve/discovery/publication code that only existed to support the parallel direct path, prove the bootstrap floor uses the same model as project code, and restate the invariants clearly.

Signal: there is one compiler definition callback, one scope-shaping expansion model, raw `FunctionSource` stays lazy behind demand, and the remaining `fz-rh2.16.4` work sits above that seam.
title: Compiler2 definition-macro closeout: delete direct publication, bootstrap honestly, and restate the invariants
